reasoning: The highest IRAT emergence score ever recorded as of August 2024 was 7.5 (for an H1N1 strain), and the latest H5N1 score at that time was 5.79. Recent updates in May 2025 show that the CDC assessed newer H5N1 viruses from California and Washington as having potential emergence scores of 5.59 and 5.21, respectively. This demonstrates a steady or slight decline in the assessed risk of H5 emergence, maintaining the "moderate risk" categorization. With the knowledge cutoff in November 2025 and only weeks remaining until the end of 2025, the chance of an unprecedented surge to a score of 8.0 (which has never occurred for any virus) is virtually nil. Additionally, any viral mutation or sustained human-to-human transmission capable of prompting such a score increase would be global breaking news, which is absent.
